embeds.title: ▶️ DM Apprentice (DA) Application
embeds.description: Players active in the server for at least 1 month may submit an application to be a DA in: <#703373885009297518>.

fields.name: 🔹 Synopsis
fields.value: DAs and DMs are required to give a synopsis of their planned session, including encounters and potential rewards, which must be reviewed/approved by two DMs or Staff members to ensure exp and loot awards are suitable for the level of the party and the difficulty of the encounter. At least one of those approvals must come from someone not participating in said table.

fields.name: 🔹 Lethality
fields.value: DAs are limited to running non-lethal arenas and tables for their first few postings, to provide them with experience in both Avrae, and encounter-balancing before they attempt to run a more complex or potentially lethal table, and accidentally TPK.

fields.name: 🔹 Kinks & Limits
fields.value: For eRP tables, you are required to include a list of kinks you intend to include to avoid potentially infringing on a player's limits and making them uncomfortable.

fields.name: 🔹 Time Zone
fields.value: Don't forget to set your time zone with `-settimezone` before posting your table to ensure players will be able to properly gauge your intended schedule. You may also use [Discord time stamps](https://sesh.fyi/timestamp/) to aid with scheduling.

embeds.title: ▶️ Magic Item Rewards
embeds.description: Magic items distributed as awards should reflect both the level of involvement and danger of the table in question. Some magic items may be deemed too powerful or disruptive to be allowed. **All** magic items must be presented to the Staff for approval prior to distribution.

fields.name: 🔹 DA Restrictions
fields.value: DAs may distribute magic items in their table only with approval of the DM staff. Under no circumstances may a DA distribute Legendary items.

fields.name: 🔹 DM Restrictions
fields.value: DMs have more leeway when distributing magic items, but must still present their probable rewards for consideration to ensure they are appropriate. DMs must obtain approval from the mod staff to distribute legendary items.

fields.name: 🔹 Artifacts
fields.value: Under no circumstances may Artifacts be given without express (and unlikely) approval from the mod staff.

fields.name: 🔹 Level Restrictions
fields.value:
The items distributed in your table should adhere to the following level restrictions from the DMG
```╔═══════════╤═══════════════════╗
║ RARITY    │ LEVEL REQUIRED    ║
╠═══════════╪═══════════════════╣
║ Common    │ >= Level 1        ║
║ Uncommon  │ >= Level 1        ║
║ Rare      │ >= Level 5        ║
║ Very Rare │ >= Level 11       ║
║ Legendary │ >= Level 17 (*)   ║
║ Artifacts │ Do Not Distribute ║
╚═══════════╧═══════════════════╝```

embeds.title: ▶️ Homebrew
embeds.description: The server's policy on homebrew remains unchanged for DAs and DMs:

fields.name: 🔹 Reflavoring
fields.value: Reflavoring existing elements is fine - you can take an existing monster or item and call it something else for the purposes of your table. Reskinning existing monsters is acceptable, but even minor changes to a monster's statblock can drastically alter their difficulty, so use of homebrewed monsters is prohibited.

fields.name: 🔹 Lewd Applications
fields.value: Existing magic items given lewd applications while keeping the same base mechanics is fine.

fields.name: 🔹 RP Items & Effects vs. Mechanics
fields.value: As with other elements of the server, we are more flexible with the homebrew of strictly RP items, provided they have zero mechanical impact. Magical plot-specific items, mcguffins, or effects which are strictly limited to the table and will not impact anything outside the table **may** be permissible, but requires prior approval from the DM staff.

embeds.title: ▶️ During the Table
embeds.description:
Should a question of ruling or inter-player conflict arise during a session, and it cannot be immediately resolved peaceably by those participating in the session, a non-participant DM or Staff member should be called in to help resolve the issue. If no resolution can be reached after a short discussion, the DA/DM running the session must make a final ruling without further discussion in order to move the session forward.
Keep it civil. This goes for all players, DAs, and DMs.

embeds.title: ▶️ After the Table
embeds.description: Players may be rewarded for monsters defeated in an encounter, plus an additional moderate discretionary award for handling traps, creative problem solving, and roleplay (eRP or otherwise). Monsters simply being in an encounter is not sufficient to award full exp for that monster (such as a nonlethal encounter where the monster bad-ends the party). Creative problem solving of an encounter without fighting (including, but not limited to, eRPing their way through an encounter) can be awarded a portion of the encounter exp.

fields.name: 🔹 RP Tables
fields.value:
For RP-focused tables (that is, tables which are centered primarily around RP, eRP or otherwise, rather than combat encounters), you may award set stipend of exp to each character. The exact amount each player receives would vary per person, based on the character's level tier. The amount listed may be awarded per real-life day of active RP and table participation.
```╔═════════╤════════════╗
║  LEVEL  │ MAX XP/DAY ║
╠═════════╪════════════╣
║   3-4   │    500xp   ║
║   5-10  │  1,000xp   ║
║  11-16  │  1,500xp   ║
║  17-20  │  2,000xp   ║
╚═════════╧════════════╝```

fields.name: 🔹 Review
fields.value: Tables may be reviewed by the DM staff for mechanical accuracy and balance of risk/reward, as well as any issues or complaints that arise. Admin & DM staff will take necessary measures to rectify any consequences of said conflict. A table's results, be it character death or rewards, may be overturned or adjusted as needed.

fields.name: 🔹 DM Awards
fields.value: Following the successful conclusion of an arena or table, a DM may use the `!dmreward arena` or `!dmreward table` alias as appropriate to earn an exp/gold reward for one of their characters. The amount of the reward is determined by the level of the receiving character and the complexity (arena or table) of what was run.

embeds.title: ▶️ Lore
embeds.description:
You have creative leeway in your table (within reason), but any lore or effects must remain confined to that table.
**__No__ server-wide lore may be affected without Mod Staff approval.** If you would like to write more wide-impacting lore for the server, it must be reviewed and approved by the Mod Staff __in advance__.

embeds.title: ▶️ Pomotion
embeds.description: DA promotion to DM will be at the discretion of the admins and existing DM staff. At minimum, a DA must run a number of successful tables, demonstrate a good sense of the mechanics and balance of the game, and remain in good standing.

fields.name: 🔹 Rules Violations
fields.value: It should go without saying that any evidence or suspicion of violating server rules, or the rules laid out here, may result in disciplinary action, including, but not limited to, removal of your status as a DM or DA.

fields.name: 🔹 Inactivity
fields.value: We all (admins/DMs/DAs/players) have real lives off of this server. However, to ensure that table slots are not being occupied by inactive people, we may occasionally inquire to gauge your interest in remaining a DA to make room for others.

fields.name: 🔹 Mod Staff Discretion
fields.value: This list is not exhaustive. There may be circumstances in which a DM or DA may warrant removal for reasons other than those listed here. The mod staff reserves the right to add to this list as necessary to ensure the server operates as smoothly as possible and with the least headaches.
